headline: Astros' Ryan Weiss: Appears headed back to bullpen
body: Weiss appears headed back to the Astros' bullpen after Kai-Wei Teng was named the team's starter for Tuesday's game in Baltimore, Matt Kawahara of the Houston Chronicle reports.

headline: Dodgers' Edgardo Henriquez: Works around wildness Sunday
body: Henriquez walked two batters and struck out one in a scoreless relief inning versus the Cubs on Sunday.

headline: Astros' Kai-Wei Teng: Tapped for start Tuesday
body: Teng will be the Astros' starting pitcher for Tuesday's game in Baltimore, Matt Kawahara of the Houston Chronicle reports.

headline: Padres' David Morgan: Hammered by D-backs on Sunday
body: Morgan retired only one batter and gave up four runs on four hits in a relief outing Sunday versus Arizona.

headline: Mets' A.J. Ewing: Elevated to Triple-A
body: The Mets promoted Ewing from Double-A Binghamton to Triple-A Syracuse on Monday, Anthony DiComo of MLB.com reports.

headline: Mets' Juan Soto: On base four times in twin bill
body: Soto went 1-for-5 with three walks across both games of Sunday's doubleheader against the Rockies.

headline: Marlins' Owen Caissie: Struggles continue Sunday
body: Caissie went 0-for-3 with a strikeout in Sunday's loss to the Giants.

headline: Braves' Ha-Seong Kim: Cleared for rehab games
body: Kim (finger) will begin a rehab assignment with Double-A Columbus this week, Grant McAuley of the Marietta Daily Journal reports.

headline: Marlins' Kyle Stowers: Makes first career start at 1B
body: Stowers got the start at first base Sunday and went 0-for-4 in a loss to the Giants.

headline: Blue Jays' Vladimir Guerrero: Three more hits Sunday
body: Guerrero went 3-for-4 with a double, a run scored and an RBI in Sunday's win over the Guardians.

headline: Braves' Michael Harris: At DH again Sunday
body: Harris went 0-for-3 with a walk and two strikeouts in Sunday's win over the Phillies.

headline: Braves' Eli White: Goes yard in Sunday's win
body: White went 1-for-4 with a two-run home run in Sunday's win over the Phillies.

headline: Athletics' Jacob Lopez: Allows one run in relief outing
body: Lopez allowed one run on a hit and two walks over one-plus inning Sunday against the Rangers. He struck out one.

headline: Braves' Ronald Acuna: Swipes sixth bag
body: Acuna went 2-for-3 with a walk, two runs scored and a stolen base in Sunday's win over the Phillies.

headline: Athletics' Justin Sterner: Picks up first win
body: Sterner (1-2) earned the win in Sunday's 2-1 victory over the Rangers. He allowed a hit and no walks while striking out two over a scoreless inning.

headline: Lucas Sims: Elects free agency
body: Sims elected to become a free agent Saturday.

headline: Brewers' Quinn Priester: Lacks control in second rehab start
body: Priester (wrist) struck out two and allowed zero hits and issued three walks across 2.1 innings in his rehab start Sunday with Triple-A Nashville.

headline: Braves' Spencer Strider: Piles up seven Ks in rehab start
body: Strider (oblique) struck out seven and allowed two earned runs on four hits and two walks across five innings Sunday in a rehab start for Triple-A Gwinnett.

headline: Mets' Austin Warren: Sent back down
body: The Mets optioned Warren to Triple-A Syracuse on Monday.

headline: Red Sox's Jarren Duran: Remains at leadoff
body: Duran batted leadoff and went 0-for-4 with a walk, a stolen base and a run scored in Sunday's 5-3 win over the Orioles.

headline: Diamondbacks' Jesus Valdez: Optioned to Triple-A
body: The Diamondbacks optioned Valdez to Triple-A Reno on Monday.

headline: Red Sox's Roman Anthony: Dropped to third in order
body: Anthony batted third and went 0-for-3 with a walk in Sunday's 5-3 win over Baltimore.

headline: Padres' Sung-Mun Song: Sent back to minors
body: The Padres optioned Song to Triple-A El Paso on Monday.

headline: Guardians' Steven Kwan: Returns from neck injury
body: Kwan started in center field and went 1-for-5 in Sunday's 4-2 loss to the Blue Jays.

headline: Reds' Nick Lodolo: Fires five scoreless in rehab start
body: Lodolo (finger) allowed two hits and struck out seven over five scoreless innings for High-A Dayton on Sunday.

headline: Diamondbacks' Nolan Arenado: Multiple hits Sunday
body: Arenado went 2-for-3 with a walk and two runs scored in Sunday's 12-7 win over the Padres.

headline: Diamondbacks' Gabriel Moreno: Return date updated
body: Moreno (oblique) is expected to rejoin the Diamondbacks during the series against the Cubs that begins Friday, Nick Piecoro of the Arizona Republic reports.

headline: Blue Jays' Jesus Sanchez: Homers, drives in three
body: Sanchez went 1-for-3 with a two-run homer and a sacrifice fly in Sunday's 4-2 victory over the Guardians.

headline: Orioles' Samuel Basallo: Homers again Sunday
body: Basallo went 2-for-4 with a solo home run in Sunday's 5-3 loss to the Red Sox.

headline: Angels' Kirby Yates: Struggling during rehab stint
body: Yates (knee) has made three minor-league rehab outings since April 17, giving up a combined seven runs on seven hits while posting a 5:1 K:BB over three innings.

headline: Angels' Grayson Rodriguez: Making progress, next step TBD
body: Rodriguez (shoulder) threw to live hitters in Arizona on Friday, and the Angels are evaluating how he came out of that session before determining the next step in his throwing program, Jeff Fletcher of the Orange County Register reports.

headline: Dodgers' Mookie Betts: May begin rehab stint within week
body: Betts (oblique) could kick off a minor-league rehab assignment as soon as the weekend of May 1-3, per MLB.com.

headline: Dodgers' Tommy Edman: Slowly building up running
body: Edman (ankle) has been facing live pitching but is still doing only straight-line running, per MLB.com.

headline: Yankees' Giancarlo Stanton: Decision on IL stint coming Monday
body: Manager Aaron Boone said Sunday that the Yankees will make a decision on Stanton, who is nursing right calf soreness, before Monday's game versus the Rangers, Gary Phillips of the New York Daily News reports.

headline: Padres' Will Wagner: Playing in extended spring games
body: Wagner (oblique) has been participating in extended spring-training games at the Padres' complex in Arizona, per MLB.com.

headline: Padres' Yuki Matsui: May rejoin Padres soon
body: Matsui (groin) may be nearing activation from the 15-day injured list, per MLB.com.

headline: Padres' Jeremiah Estrada: Begins rehab stint
body: Estrada (elbow) kicked off a minor-league rehab assignment with Single-A Lake Elsinore on Friday, per MLB.com.

headline: Yankees' Anthony Volpe: Likely to return during coming week
body: Volpe (shoulder) is slated to join the Yankees "between Wednesday and Thursday," Francys Romero of BeisbolFR.com reports.

headline: Rockies' Troy Johnston: RBI in four straight games
body: Johnston went 2-for-7 with two RBI and a walk across both games of Sunday's doubleheader against the Mets.

headline: Rockies' Edouard Julien: Strong showing across doubleheader
body: Julien went 3-for-8 with three runs scored and a walk across both games of Sunday's doubleheader against the Mets.

headline: Phillies' Kyle Schwarber: Smashes ninth homer Sunday
body: Schwarber went 2-for-3 with a solo home run and a walk in Sunday's 6-2 loss to Atlanta.

headline: Astros' Isaac Paredes: Continues to heat up with homer
body: Paredes went 2-for-4 with a home run, three RBI and three runs scored in Sunday's 7-4 win over the Yankees.

headline: Athletics' Carlos Cortes: Keeps rolling with first triple
body: Cortes went 3-for-4 with a two-run triple in Sunday's 2-1 win over the Rangers.

headline: Angels' Josh Lowe: Knocks three hits Sunday
body: Lowe went 3-for-4 with a run scored in Sunday's 11-9 extra-inning loss to the Royals.

headline: Athletics' Jack Perkins: Tallies second six-out save
body: Perkins notched the save Sunday against the Rangers, allowing one hit and no walks in two scoreless innings. He struck out three.

headline: Angels' Zach Neto: Three-hit effort Sunday
body: Neto went 3-for-5 with two doubles, a walk, two RBI and two runs scored in Sunday's 11-9 extra-inning loss to the Royals.

headline: Angels' Mike Trout: Goes yard in loss
body: Trout went 1-for-5 with a two-run home run and a walk in Sunday's 11-9 extra-inning loss to the Royals.

headline: Rangers' Kumar Rocker: Tough-luck loss Sunday
body: Rocker (1-2) took the loss Sunday against the Athletics, allowing two runs on six hits and two walks in six innings. He struck out three.

headline: Angels' Drew Pomeranz: Blows save chance
body: Pomeranz took a blown save in Sunday's 11-9 extra-inning loss to the Royals, allowing one run on one hit over one-third of an inning. He didn't strike out or walk a batter.
